text: Machine learning is a field where computers learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. One common type is supervised learning. Here, the computer is given training data, which includes examples with correct answers. For instance, you might show it many pictures of cats and dogs, each labelled correctly. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is like a set of rules it creates. Once trained, the model can make predictions on new, unseen pictures. After predicting, we check its accuracy by seeing how many predictions were correct. Better training data usually leads to more accurate models.
